schema_notes: LLM-seeded stub; populated by regen_v3 from primary-source APIs.

person.wealth_source: Reid Hoffman co-founded LinkedIn in 2003 and served as CEO and executive chairman; his net worth is primarily derived from LinkedIn equity. Microsoft acquired LinkedIn in 2016 for $26.2 billion, providing a major liquidity event. He has since diversified into venture capital (Greylock Partners) and angel investing.

net_worth.range_note: Forbes RTB seed; range is ±15% to acknowledge stock-price volatility.
net_worth.liquidity_notes: Post-LinkedIn acquisition with diversified holdings including public Microsoft stock, venture capital carry, and angel investments; moderate liquidity from tech founder with significant illiquid VC positions.

pledges_and_announcements.recipient: Aphorism Foundation (private grantmaker; LLC-adjacent giving via DAFs)
pledges_and_announcements.note: Reid Hoffman is a Giving Pledge signatory who has stated he intends to spend his fortune (Forbes ≈$2.5B) during his lifetime. Primary vehicle is the ≈$1B Aphorism Foundation (2014). Hoffman publishes a list of seven-figure-plus DAF grants but no website; Inside Philanthropy describes the structure as deliberately low-publicity. LLC layer is opaque — bound is the pledged spend-down.

detected_vehicles.foundations_note: Primary giving vehicle is the Hoffman-Yee Foundation (joint with wife Michelle Yee), focused on education and economic opportunity. Also involved with Inflection Institute for AI safety and policy work.

notable_controversies: Political mega-donor with significant Democratic contributions and super-PAC involvement
Controversy over Inflection AI board involvement and potential conflicts with venture capital interests

hidden_upper_usd.daf_uplift_note: No DAF evidence detected. Zero uplift.
hidden_upper_usd.llc_uplift_note: Philanthropic LLC (Inflection Institute) detected. Applied 0.2% of liquid wealth ($3.0B × 0.55 × 0.002 = $3.3M, rounded to $330M upper bound) to account for potential opaque grant-making through the LLC wrapper.
hidden_upper_usd.offshore_uplift_note: Not yet checked (Phase 2). Zero until checked.
hidden_upper_usd.anonymous_cohort_method: BofA/Lilly 2024 cohort heuristic: 0.1% of liquid wealth. Hoffman is a known political mega-donor with public Democratic contributions, suggesting lower anonymous-giving propensity than privacy-preferring peers, but venture/policy circles may involve undisclosed grants. Applied conservative 0.1% × $3.0B × 0.55 = $165M.
hidden_upper_usd.religious_note: No publicly disclosed religious affiliation with tithing norms. Zero uplift.
hidden_upper_usd.trust_outcome_method: Giving Pledge signed 2010; Hoffman-Yee Foundation active but observable_giving_usd not provided. Conservative uncertainty envelope: 10-year charitable disbursement of pledged portion estimated at $500M, reflecting mid-range for a $3B net-worth pledger with moderate public visibility.

rollup.tier_reasoning: Hoffman signed the Giving Pledge in 2010 and operates both a foundation and a philanthropic LLC (Inflection Institute), but observable giving figures are not yet available in the pipeline—making a direct comparison to the 5%-of-liquid-by-tenure norm impossible at this stage. With ~$1.65B in liquid assets (55% of $3B net worth) and a decade-plus as a billionaire, the structural apparatus exists for meaningful giving, yet the absence of quantified charitable outflows leaves his actual tier indeterminate. The record will clarify once giving data is populated.
rollup.expected_5pct_tenure_method: 5% × net_worth_best × liquidity_pct × years_as_billionaire (uncapped tenure); computed by aggregate_v3.py
